claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for your child.

Let me ask you some questions:

**What is your child's name and how old are they?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex, and they're 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Since Alex is 18 years old, their eligibility depends on whether they're in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, in sixth form, college, A-levels, or approved training courses)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in approved education. They're studying for their A-levels at sixth form.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you! Since Alex is 18 and in approved education (studying A-levels), they may still qualify.

Now I need to understand your relationship to Alex and your living situation:

**Do you live with Alex, or if not, do you contribute financially towards their upkeep (such as food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! And one more important question:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me:

- **Alex is 18 years old** and studying A-levels at sixth form, which counts as approved education
- **Alex lives with you** in the UK (Manchester)
- You are responsible for Alex

According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. However, you'll **still qualify if your child is under 20** and they stay in approved education or training.

Since Alex is 18 (under 20) and is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form), you should be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me provide my formal assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Since Alex is 18, under 20, and studying A-levels at sixth form (which is approved education), and lives with you in the UK, you meet the eligibility criteria.

**Important things to remember:**
- Your eligibility will continue as long as Alex remains in approved education and is under 20
- If Alex finishes their A-levels or leaves education, your Child Benefit may stop
- If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge

If you haven't already claimed, you can make a claim for Child Benefit. If you have any other questions, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and studying A-levels at sixth form, which is approved education. Under Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for a child under 16, but eligibility extends to children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex meets this criteria as they are under 20 and in approved education. Additionally, Alex lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), establishing that the claimant is responsible for the child. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old child Alex, who is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form). Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit under the extended eligibility rules for 16-19 year olds in education or training.
